detail_raw_text:
回上一頁	國立金門大學	 
 	教學綱要	 

部別 : 日間部學士班	112學年度第2學期	列印日期 : 2026/05/24
科目名稱 : 航空英文 (English for Airline Industry)	開課班級 : 應英四乙	學　　分 : 2.0	授課時數 : 2.0
授課教師 : 周晏安	必選修 : 選修	 	 


1.教學目標
In order to prepare students to become the ground crews or flight attendants for their future career, students will be able to..... 1) know the English terms, abbreviations, city codes, carrier codes of countries all over the world. 2) be familiar with the English vocabucalries, expressions, or talks on the ground or in flights with the travelers from different countries. 3) be well-prepared for pre-interviewing in English autobiography and resume for their carrers as ground crews or flight attendants through job analysis and written exam. 


2.教學綱要
The theme-based contents in this course for working preparations as ground crews or flight attendants will be listed as below.
1. English terms, abbreviations, city codes, carrier codes of countries in aviation career.
2) English vocabularies, expressions, annoucements, talks and doalgues used for flight attendants/ground crews.
3) The English formats of autobiography and resume as ground crews/flight attendants and job interviewing in English. 


3.教科書
1	書名 : 自編教材　
出版日期 : 年 月 
作者 : 　出版社 : 　版本 :  


無參考書資料。

※請遵守智慧財產權觀念，依著作權法規定，教科書及教材不得非法影印與使用盜版軟體。

5.教學進度表
週次	日期	內容	備註
1	
2024/02/18─2024/02/24
	Class Orientation
Unit 1 空服與地勤工作總論 Getting to know the Profession

	Part 1 地勤工作的英語 Ground Crew Daily English
1-1機場櫃台Check-In & Baggage Services

6.成績評定及課堂要求
Class attendance and participation 30% (15% Class attendance: active participation, overall preparedness, skill development, etc.; 15% In-class pair/group involvement) Quizzes 20% Mid-term Exam 25% Final oral test 10% Final Written Resume (CV), Self-Introduction, Cover Letter 15% 
1.We are all responsible for maintaining a good and clean learning environment for the class. 2. When you enter the classroom, make sure you have already set your cellphones on silent mode. 3. Please respect each other all the time.  
Extra credit with blue card for : 1. Early bird 2. Active learners with volunteer involvement
